messages.content: Build a pure Decider for a GalleryShow aggregate in TypeScript. Export initialState, decide, and evolve using discriminated unions for Command, Event, and State. Throw DomainError for invalid transitions, and use an exhaustive never default.

Write an event-sourced Decider for a Painting aggregate. Strict types, no any, exhaustive switch with never default, and reject authenticating a painting that has already been rejected by an appraiser.

Explain briefly how a Decider captures the lifecycle of a Sculpture aggregate, then show a working typed implementation with receive, clean, display, and deaccession commands.

A Decider bundles `initialState`, a pure `decide(command, state)` that enforces invariants and emits events, and a pure `evolve(state, event)` that folds those events back into state. The pair is deterministic, side-effect-free, and sits at the core of the command path; the handler is the only orchestration layer around it.
Write a Decider for an ArtAppraisal aggregate. Commands: request, assign, submit, countersign. Use typed DomainError. Reject submitting before an appraiser is assigned.
Build a Decider for an Exhibition aggregate with commands curate, schedule, start, end. Strict types, no any, typed DomainError, and exhaustive switches with never defaults.
What's the event-sourcing way to encode a Curation aggregate as a Decider with commands propose, approve, and retract? Show a compact example with a typed DomainError.
Write a CommandHandler for an ArtAuction aggregate. It should load the stream, reduce via evolve, call decide, and appendToStream with the last-known revision. Include a local EventStore interface.

How do I write a CommandHandler for a LoanAgreement that loads events, decides, and appends with expectedRevision? Keep the handler free of business logic and show a minimal local EventStore interface.
Write an Effect-wrapped CommandHandler for an Installation aggregate. Use Effect.gen to orchestrate readStream, reduce, decide, and appendToStream. Keep the core decide/evolve pure.
Add a dry-run mode to this v5 CommandHandler for an Artifact aggregate: {snippet}. When dryRun is true, compute the events via decide but skip appendToStream. Keep the EventStore interface local.

Build a TypeScript projection that folds GalleryShow events into a GalleryShowSummaryView. Include a project(view, event) function, an emptyView literal, and ignore events the view does not care about.
What's the event-sourcing way to build a read model that totals hammer prices per lot for ArtAuction events? Show a minimal example with project and an initial view.

Explain briefly how a read-model projection differs from an aggregate evolve, then implement a projection that tracks active Exhibitions with their themes.

A projection folds events into a flat, query-friendly view across many streams and never rejects events; events it doesn't care about return the view unchanged. An aggregate `evolve` in contrast folds events from a single stream into that aggregate's own state, which `decide` then inspects to enforce invariants. Projections are consumer-specific; `evolve` is authoritative.

Write an event-sourced projection that folds Donor events into a per-donor total-gifts view. Constraints: pure project function, emptyView literal, ignore unrelated events by returning the view unchanged.

Build a versioned Snapshot type for an ArtAuction aggregate with CURRENT_VERSION, two upgrader functions v1Tov2 and v2Tov3, and a loader that runs upgraders in order. Assert that the post-upgrade value matches the current state type.
How do I version a Painting snapshot with upgraders? Show a Snapshot<S> type with CURRENT_VERSION and two upgrade steps that migrate older shapes forward.
Fix this v4-style snapshot loader for a Sculpture aggregate to use a proper Snapshot<S> versioned upgrade chain with CURRENT_VERSION and per-step upgraders.

Build a TypeScript optimistic-concurrency appendToStream helper for a Curation aggregate. Use a local EventStore interface with expectedRevision: bigint | "no_stream" | "any" and surface a typed ConcurrencyConflict when the server reports a mismatch.
Write an Effect-wrapped optimistic-append helper for an ArtAuction aggregate. Translate store conflicts into a tagged ConcurrencyConflict failure and keep the EventStore interface local.
Refactor this inline append call for a Donor stream to use expectedRevision with a local EventStore interface, and translate a conflict into a typed ConcurrencyConflict.

Build a resumable subscription for a Painting projection in TypeScript. Use a CheckpointStore interface with read/write, wrap the top-level for await, and write the checkpoint only after the projection is persisted.
How do I build a resumable subscription for an Exhibition read model that survives restarts? Show a CheckpointStore interface and a runSubscription loop.
Show a resumable subscription for a Sculpture projection using Effect.gen. The checkpoint is written only after the view is persisted so crashes cannot skip events.
Build a generic aggregateStream helper in TypeScript that replays an AsyncIterable of events into aggregate state using evolve. Show it applied to a LoanAgreement.
What's the event-sourcing way to rebuild a Curation aggregate from a stream using aggregateStream? Show a minimal generic fold and an evolve that handles all events.
Write a pure reducer for a GalleryShow aggregate using evolve. Fold events to reconstruct state and expose a typed aggregate(events) helper that uses when as a call.
Write an event-sourced evolve/when reducer for an Artifact aggregate with events accessioned, loanedOut, returned, decommissioned. Constraints: strict types, no any, exhaustive never default.
Fix this v4-ish ArtAppraisal reducer so it uses an exhaustive evolve with a never default and a typed discriminated Event union.

Build a read-model rebuild loop for an Exhibition view. Accept a from revision, a batchSize, a project function, and a persist callback. Read events in batches, advance a cursor, and allow resumption after a crash.
How do I wipe and re-project a Donor read model from revision 0 with progress reporting? Show a resumable chunked rebuild loop.

Build a minimal CQRS boundary for a GalleryShow aggregate. handleCommand writes via Decider + appendToStream; queryView reads from a read store. Note that the read side is eventually consistent. Do not share a state type across the boundary.

Explain briefly how CQRS separates writes and reads for a Donor aggregate, then show a minimal typed implementation with handleCommand and queryView.

CQRS splits the command side (Decider + appendToStream with expectedRevision) from the query side (a read store populated by a projection). The two sides share the Event DU but not the state type: the write side's aggregate state enforces invariants; the read side's view is shaped for the UI. The read store is eventually consistent with the event store, so queries can lag behind writes.

Write an Effect-wrapped outbox appender for LoanAgreement events. It should atomically append events to the stream and insert outbox rows in one unit of work. A separate worker polls and marks rows as published (not implemented here).
Refactor this bare Snapshot usage into a proper Snapshot<S> with CURRENT_VERSION and an upgradeSnapshot chain for a GalleryShow aggregate.
What's the event-sourcing way to build a CommandHandler helper for an ArtAppraisal that loads the stream, calls decide, and uses appendToStream with expectedRevision?
